Empathy is feeling WITH someone — sharing or mirroring their emotion from the inside; sympathy is feeling FOR someone — acknowledging and caring about their suffering from the outside without sharing the feeling itself.

A refrigerator stays cold using a refrigerant cycle: a compressor pressurizes the refrigerant so it releases heat outside through coils, then it expands and evaporates inside, absorbing heat from the food compartment and carrying it away.

Muscles get stronger through progressive overload: resistance training causes tiny tears in muscle fibers, and the body repairs them thicker and stronger during rest, given enough protein and recovery.

The night sky is dark because your side of Earth faces away from the Sun, so no sunlight is scattering through the atmosphere toward you; distant stars are far too faint and sparse to light up the whole sky.

Airplanes fly because their forward speed pushes air over the wings, whose shape and slight upward tilt deflect air downward and create lower pressure on top; this produces lift that overcomes the plane's weight, while engines supply the thrust.

A meteor is the streak of light or shooting star made when a space rock burns up in the atmosphere; the rock in space is a meteoroid, and any piece that survives and lands on the ground is a meteorite.
Goosebumps happen when tiny muscles at the base of each body hair contract and pull the hair upright; it is a leftover reflex from furry ancestors, triggered by cold or strong emotion.
Soap cleans because each soap molecule has one end that bonds to water and one that bonds to grease, so it surrounds oily dirt, lifts it off the surface, and lets it rinse away in water.

Mass is the amount of matter in an object and is the same everywhere, while weight is the force of gravity acting on that mass and changes with location — on the Moon you weigh less, but your mass is unchanged.

The Moon has phases because we see different amounts of its sunlit half as it orbits Earth over about 29.5 days; the Moon makes no light of its own and only reflects the Sun, so our changing viewing angle creates the phases.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 Fahrenheit) at sea level, the point where its vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure; at high altitude the thinner air lowers that boiling point.
An acid donates hydrogen ions (protons) and has a pH below 7, while a base accepts them and has a pH above 7; when an acid and base react they neutralize each other to form water and a salt.
Gravity is the attractive force every mass exerts on every other mass — stronger for larger masses and shorter distances; it holds planets in orbit and makes dropped objects fall toward Earth.

Consciousness is the state of subjective awareness — having an inner, first-person experience of sensations, thoughts, and a sense of self; explaining how physical brain activity produces that felt experience is called the hard problem of consciousness.

Plants drink water through their roots, which absorb it from the soil; it then rises through tube-like xylem vessels, pulled upward as water evaporates from the leaves in a process called transpiration.

Volcanoes erupt when molten rock called magma, lighter than the surrounding rock and full of dissolved gas, rises through cracks in the crust and bursts out as lava, ash, and gas once the pressure is released.

The boiling point of water is 100 degrees Celsius (212 Fahrenheit) at sea level, the temperature where its vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure; at high altitude the thinner air lowers that boiling point.

A stock is a share of ownership in a company that rises or falls in value and may pay dividends, while a bond is a loan to a company or government that pays fixed interest and returns your principal at maturity; stocks carry more risk and higher potential return.

The human eye sees color using cone cells in the retina, which come in three types tuned to red, green, and blue light; the brain blends their signals into the full spectrum, while rod cells handle dim light and motion in shades of gray.

A black market is illegal trade in goods or services that are banned, restricted, stolen, or untaxed, operating outside government regulation and taxation; it tends to appear wherever something is prohibited or in scarce supply.

Latitude lines run east-west and measure how far north or south of the equator you are (0 to 90 degrees), while longitude lines run north-south and measure how far east or west of the prime meridian you are (0 to 180 degrees).

Metal rusts when iron reacts with oxygen and water to form flaky iron oxide; this electrochemical corrosion keeps exposing fresh metal, which is why iron is painted or galvanized to seal out air and moisture.

Magnets work because the spins of many of their electrons line up in the same direction, creating a magnetic field; opposite poles attract and like poles repel, and a moving magnetic field can push electric charges, linking magnetism to electricity.

Communism is a system in which the community or state owns all property and goods are shared by need, aiming for a classless society, while socialism is broader and often democratic, with collective ownership of major industries but room for private property and markets.

Osmosis is the movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from where solute is dilute to where it is concentrated, balancing the concentration on both sides; it is how plant roots and living cells take in water.

Cats purr by rapidly vibrating the muscles of their voice box as they breathe in and out; they purr not only when content but also to self-soothe, and the vibrations may help healing when they are stressed or hurt.

A credit score is a number, usually 300 to 850, that summarizes how reliably a person repays debt based on payment history, amounts owed, credit age, and new credit; lenders use it to decide whether to lend and at what interest rate.

A hurricane is a huge, slow, long-lasting storm that forms over warm ocean water and can span hundreds of miles, while a tornado is a small, violent, short-lived funnel of rotating air over land; hurricanes are far larger but tornadoes pack faster local winds.

WiFi works by sending data as radio waves between a wireless router and your device; the router connects to the internet by cable and broadcasts on set frequencies (2.4 and 5 GHz) that your device's antenna receives and decodes.

The French Revolution (1789-1799) overthrew the French monarchy and feudal privilege, driven by inequality, debt, and Enlightenment ideals; it produced the Declaration of the Rights of Man, the Reign of Terror, and ultimately Napoleon's rise to power.

Deflation is a sustained fall in the general price level, the opposite of inflation; it can be dangerous because people delay purchases expecting lower prices, which slows the economy and raises the real weight of debt.

A comet is an icy body that grows a glowing tail of gas and dust when it nears the Sun, while an asteroid is a rocky or metallic body with no tail; most asteroids orbit between Mars and Jupiter and comets come from the icy outer solar system.
Onions make you cry because cutting them releases a sulfur compound into the air that reacts with the moisture in your eyes to form a mild acid; your eyes water to flush the irritant away.

A tariff is a tax a government places on imported goods, making foreign products more expensive to protect domestic industry or raise revenue; tariffs can also raise prices for consumers and trigger trade wars.

Anesthesia works by using drugs to block nerve signals: local anesthesia numbs a small area, while general anesthesia acts on the brain to produce unconsciousness, stillness, and loss of pain and memory during surgery.

A fever is caused by the immune system raising the body's temperature set-point to fight infection; chemicals called pyrogens tell the brain's hypothalamus to warm the body, which slows pathogen growth and speeds immune response.

The placebo effect is feeling better from a treatment with no active ingredient because you expect to improve, while the nocebo effect is the opposite — feeling worse purely from expecting harm; both reveal the mind's influence over the body.

A seed grows into a plant through germination: it soaks up water and swells, the embryo's root grows down for water and anchorage, the shoot pushes up toward light, and once leaves open the plant photosynthesizes to feed itself.

A republic is a state where power rests with elected representatives and a non-monarch head of state, while a democracy is rule by the people; most modern democracies are also republics that govern through elected representatives under a constitution.

The ocean looks blue because water absorbs the longer red and orange wavelengths of sunlight and scatters the shorter blue wavelengths back to our eyes; deeper, clearer water appears a more intense blue.

The digestive system breaks food into absorbable nutrients: the mouth and stomach break it down mechanically and chemically, the small intestine absorbs the nutrients into the blood, and the large intestine reclaims water before waste leaves the body.

Speed is how fast something moves — a scalar with only magnitude like 60 km/h — while velocity is speed in a specific direction, a vector; a car circling a track at constant speed has constantly changing velocity.

A monopoly exists when a single seller controls an entire market with no real competition, letting it raise prices and cut output; because monopolies reduce choice and efficiency, governments often regulate or break them up.

Antidepressants work mainly by raising the availability of mood-related brain chemicals like serotonin and norepinephrine; SSRIs block serotonin reabsorption, and the benefit builds over weeks as neural circuits gradually adapt.

A crocodile has a longer V-shaped snout and shows teeth from both jaws when its mouth is shut, while an alligator has a wider U-shaped snout that hides its lower teeth; crocodiles handle saltwater better and tend to be more aggressive.

Dark energy is a mysterious form of energy making up about 68 percent of the universe that acts as a repulsive force driving the accelerating expansion of space; it is distinct from dark matter, which adds gravity but does not push space apart.

We cry when sad as an emotional release thought to shed stress hormones and to signal distress to others, drawing comfort and support; emotional tears differ chemically from the basic tears that keep the eyes moist.
